title: festuca parvigluma f. breviaristata
intro: Festuca parvigluma f. breviaristata is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as meadow fescue. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Festuca parvigluma f. breviaristata include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a natural antiseptic. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it may also be dried and used in herbal infusions or added to topical creams and ointments.
